steps.title: Spot the causal relationship
steps.content: The voters were impressed by the album, then they awarded it the award. The transition must signal that the award follows as a natural consequence of the impression.

steps.title: Match the transition to the logic
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is cause-effect. Sentence 1 establishes that voters were impressed by the album; Sentence 2 states they awarded the group the prize. The award follows as a direct consequence of the impression. The blank needs a transition signaling that result.
  • Choice A — "In contrast,": signals opposition — the two sentences are not opposed; awarding a prize to an impressive album is consistent, not contradictory. ✗
  • Choice B — "Accordingly,": signals "as a result" — the award follows logically from the voters being impressed. ✓
  • Choice C — "Meanwhile,": signals simultaneity or a parallel event happening at the same time — the award is a consequence, not a concurrent event. ✗
  • Choice D — "Nevertheless,": signals "despite that" — there is nothing to overcome; the award is the expected outcome of admiration. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
